quiz.title: Unit 9 Quiz 5, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Percent Yield and Product Mass Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Percent Yield Calculations, Theoretical vs Actual Yield, Stoichiometry, Limiting Reactants, Mass of Products

questions.question: In a reaction with theoretical yield 50.0 g, if 42.5 g is obtained, the percent yield is:
A synthesis has theoretical yield 25.0 g and percent yield 72.0%. What is the actual yield?
questions.explanation:
Actual yield = theoretical yield × (percent yield ÷ 100)
= 25.0 × 0.720 = 18.0 g

questions.question: If 15.0 g product is obtained with 60.0% yield, what was the theoretical yield?
questions.explanation:
Theoretical yield = actual yield ÷ (percent yield ÷ 100)
= 15.0 ÷ 0.600 = 25.0 g

questions.question: For the reaction 2H₂ + O₂ → 2H₂O, 10.0 g H₂ produces 80.0 g H₂O. What is the percent yield?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass H₂ = 2.016 g/mol, H₂O = 18.02 g/mol
Moles H₂ = 10.0/2.016 = 4.96 mol
Theoretical H₂O = 4.96 mol (1:1 mole ratio from H₂ to H₂O)
Mass theoretical = 4.96 × 18.02 = 89.4 g
Percent yield = (80.0 ÷ 89.4) × 100% = 89.5% ≈ 88.8%

questions.question: A reaction produces 12.4 g product with 82.0% yield. The theoretical yield is:
questions.explanation:
Theoretical yield = actual yield ÷ (percent yield ÷ 100)
= 12.4 ÷ 0.820 = 15.12 g ≈ 15.1 g

questions.question: Theoretical yield for a reaction is calculated to be 68.0 g. If 51.0 g is isolated, the percent yield is:
A student obtains 8.20 g of product when the theoretical yield is 10.0 g. The percent yield is:
For CaCO₃ → CaO + CO₂, 100. g CaCO₃ yields 48.0 g CaO. What is the percent yield?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass CaCO₃ = 100.09 g/mol, CaO = 56.08 g/mol
Moles CaCO₃ = 100/100.09 = 0.999 mol
Theoretical CaO = 0.999 mol
Mass theoretical = 0.999 × 56.08 = 56.0 g
Percent yield = (48.0 ÷ 56.0) × 100% = 85.7%

questions.question: ___________ is the amount of product actually obtained from an experiment.
questions.explanation: Actual yield is the amount of product actually obtained from an experiment. It is typically less than the theoretical yield due to experimental losses, incomplete reactions, or side reactions.

questions.question: In 2Na + 2H₂O → 2NaOH + H₂, 4.60 g Na and 3.60 g H₂O react. What mass of NaOH forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Na = 23 g/mol, H₂O = 18 g/mol, NaOH = 40.00 g/mol
Moles Na = 4.60/23 = 0.200 mol (needs 0.200 mol H₂O)
Moles H₂O = 3.60/18 = 0.200 mol
Exact stoichiometry
NaOH produced = 0.200 mol
Mass = 0.200 × 40.00 = 8.00 g

questions.question: ___________ is the maximum amount of product calculated from stoichiometry.
questions.explanation: Theoretical yield is the maximum amount of product that could be formed from given amounts of reactants, based on stoichiometric calculations. Actual yield is what is experimentally obtained, and percentage yield is the ratio of actual to theoretical yield expressed as a percentage.

questions.question: For 2Al + 6HCl → 2AlCl₃ + 3H₂, 5.40 g Al and 21.9 g HCl react. What mass of H₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass Al = 27 g/mol, HCl = 36.46 g/mol, H₂ = 2.016 g/mol
Moles Al = 5.40/27 = 0.200 mol (needs 0.600 mol HCl)
Moles HCl = 21.9/36.46 = 0.600 mol
Exact stoichiometry
H₂ produced = 0.200 × (3/2) = 0.300 mol
Mass = 0.300 × 2.016 = 0.605 g ≈ 0.600 g

questions.question: Given 2HCl + Na₂CO₃ → 2NaCl + H₂O + CO₂, 14.6 g HCl and 21.2 g Na₂CO₃ react. What mass of CO₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass HCl = 36.46 g/mol, Na₂CO₃ = 106 g/mol, CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Moles HCl = 14.6/36.46 = 0.400 mol (needs 0.200 mol Na₂CO₃)
Moles Na₂CO₃ = 21.2/106 = 0.200 mol
Exact stoichiometry
CO₂ produced = 0.200 mol
Mass = 0.200 × 44.01 = 8.80 g

questions.question: For C₃H₈ + 5O₂ → 3CO₂ + 4H₂O, 22.0 g C₃H₈ and 80.0 g O₂ react. What mass of CO₂ forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass C₃H₈ = 44.10 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol, CO₂ = 44.01 g/mol
Moles C₃H₈ = 22.0/44.10 = 0.499 mol (needs 2.50 mol O₂)
Moles O₂ = 80.0/32 = 2.50 mol
Exact stoichiometry
CO₂ produced = 0.499 × 3 = 1.497 mol
Mass = 1.497 × 44.01 = 65.9 g ≈ 66.0 g

questions.question: In 4NH₃ + 5O₂ → 4NO + 6H₂O, 17.0 g NH₃ and 40.0 g O₂ react. What mass of NO forms?
questions.explanation:
Molar mass NH₃ = 17.03 g/mol, O₂ = 32 g/mol, NO = 30.01 g/mol
Moles NH₃ = 17.0/17.03 = 0.998 mol (needs 1.25 mol O₂)
Moles O₂ = 40.0/32 = 1.25 mol
Exact stoichiometry
NO produced = 0.998 mol
Mass = 0.998 × 30.01 = 29.9 g ≈ 30.0 g

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered percent yield and product mass calculations! 🧪📊
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the percent yield formula and limiting reactant identification.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on converting between actual yield, theoretical yield, and percent yield.
scoreMessages.0: Please review percent yield calculations, stoichiometry, and mass-to-mole conversions.
